description:
You are the backup administrator for a small workstation.  
A developer keeps all of her work under the directory

    /home/user/data/

At the moment the directory already contains two source files:

    /home/user/data/alpha.txt
    /home/user/data/beta.txt

Your job is to archive this directory and register the archive in a log file.

REQUIREMENTS
1. Create the directory /home/user/archive/ if it does not yet exist.  
2. Inside that directory create a **compressed** tar archive that contains everything found under /home/user/data/ (the directory itself must not be included, only its contents).  
   • The archive must be called exactly  
        /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz  
   • The archive must be a gzip-compressed tar file (i.e. the file command should report “… gzip compressed …”).  
3. After the archive is created, append **one single line** to the file  

        /home/user/archive/backup_record.log  

   The line must be written in CSV format with the three fields shown below and **no extra spaces**:

        data_backup.tar.gz,<SIZE_IN_BYTES>,<SHA256_CHECKSUM>

   where  
     • <SIZE_IN_BYTES> is the exact decimal byte-size of /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz as returned by the command `stat -c%s /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz`  
     • <SHA256_CHECKSUM> is the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz as produced by `sha256sum` (only the hash, not the filename).  

   Example of the correct record format (values shown are illustrative, **do not copy them**):

        data_backup.tar.gz,1488,3e92938c5d9cdb7a0bdd3f11344d8d5a60fb0c93f8b6d4c6ab0c67e89a77d022

4. The archive file and the log file must be world-readable (chmod 644) but only the owner needs write permission.  
5. Do **not** modify the contents of /home/user/data/. The original files must stay in place.

DELIVERABLES THAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY TESTED
• /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz exists, is a gzip-compressed tar, and contains exactly the two plaintext files alpha.txt and beta.txt (with their original relative names only).  
• /home/user/archive/backup_record.log exists and ends with one line that matches the format specified above, whose SIZE_IN_BYTES and SHA256_CHECKSUM correspond to the actual data_backup.tar.gz that you produced.  
• File permissions on both files are 644 (rw-r--r--).

The whole task can be completed with two or three standard shell commands (tar, sha256sum, stat, mkdir, echo, etc.). No elevated privileges are required.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

1. Directory /home/user/data/ already exists (0755).  
2. Files and their exact contents and permissions:  
   • /home/user/data/alpha.txt (0644)  
       Line 1: "Hello Alpha\n"  
   • /home/user/data/beta.txt  (0644)  
       Line 1: "Beta line1\n"  
       Line 2: "Beta line2\n"  
3. Directory /home/user/archive/ does NOT exist.  
4. No file called /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz or /home/user/archive/backup_record.log exists.

Verification logic used by the test suite:

A. Confirm /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz exists and is readable.  
B. Run `tar tzf` on the archive: the list of paths must be exactly

       alpha.txt
       beta.txt

   (no leading "./" and no sub-directories).  
C. Compute its size in bytes with `stat -c%s` → SIZE.  
D. Compute its SHA-256 checksum with `sha256sum` → HASH.  
E. Read the last non-empty line of /home/user/archive/backup_record.log → LINE.  
   • The line must match the regex  
       ^data_backup\.tar\.gz,[0-9]+,[0-9a-f]{64}$  
   • Split on the commas and compare the second and third fields to SIZE and HASH found in steps C and D. They must be identical.  
F. Use `stat -c %a` to ensure both /home/user/archive/data_backup.tar.gz and /home/user/archive/backup_record.log have octal mode 644.

The task passes only if all checks (A–F) succeed.
name: task_000000_dcdd53fb
